examples: Be consistent with your study habits.
Her performance has been very consistent.
context: I honestly hate sitting all day so whenever I get the chance I like to take a short walk outside and get some fresh air. These days while I walk I like to listen to the MP3 files from my Korean class. I'm trying to make it a daily habit and I've definitely made some progress with my listening lately. It's such an easy way to stay consistent with my studies.

examples: I love staying in my cozy bed on rainy days.
They have a cozy little apartment.
context: I always study best at cafes. Maybe it's the background noise or just the cozy atmosphere.

examples: I'm definitely coming to the party.
This is definitely the best pizza I've ever had.
You should definitely try the cheesecake.
context: I honestly hate sitting all day so whenever I get the chance I like to take a short walk outside and get some fresh air. These days while I walk I like to listen to the MP3 files from my Korean class. I'm trying to make it a daily habit and I've definitely made some progress with my listening lately. It's such an easy way to stay consistent with my studies.

Lately I've been doing shadowing while walking on my walking pad. Shadowing is when you listen and repeat right after the speaker. I usually play a short audio clip and try to mimic the speaker's pronunciation and intonation as closely as possible. It's definitely not easy but honestly this part of studying is really fun for me.

examples: I planned a detailed itinerary for the trip.
The tour guide handed out the itinerary.
context: Oh, I think it's itinerary schedule. Am I right?

examples: Focus on pronunciation, not just grammar.
context: First things first, I'm going to set up my desk for studying. I like having a space that's clean and simple, so I'm going to clear everything off of my desk. Thank you. I just like to keep a notebook and pen and a water bottle on my desk. I will also be using my computer, so I'm going to put on my blue light glasses. Well, do I look nerdy? I'm going to start with vocabulary because it's what I struggle with the most. I have a terrible memory and forget things so easily. So I use an app called Quizlet to help me memorize key vocabulary. While making flashcards, I like to listen to the pronunciation and practice saying the words out loud so that I can get used to saying them naturally.
It shows dual subtitles and lets you pause and review words instantly. You can click on each word to see the definition and listen to its pronunciation.
